name: Rainbow Ribbons Refrigerator Cookies
recipeInstructions:
4 hours or 1 week ahead:
1. Combine first six ingredients in a large mixing bowl. Beat on low speed until well blended. Divide dough into thirds; place each in a separate bowl.
2. In a cup, stir instant espresso and 2 teaspoons hot tap water until dissolved. Stir espresso mixture and cocoa into one third of the dough; knead with your hands until well blended. To the second third of the dough, add anise extract and enough green food coloring to tint the dough a pretty green; knead until well blended. To the remaining dough, add enough red food coloring to tint it pink; knead until well blended. Shape each portion into a ball; wrap in plastic wrap and refrigerate for 1 hour or until firm enough to handle (or place dough in the freezer for 30 minutes).
3. On a sheet of waxed paper with a floured rolling pin, roll the pink dough into a 12 x 7 1/2-inch rectangle. On another sheet of waxed paper, roll the chocolate dough into a 12 x 7 1/2-inch rectangle. On another sheet of waxed paper, roll the green dough into a 12 x 7 1/2-inch rectangle. Invert the chocolate dough onto the pink dough; invert the green dough onto the chocolate dough.
4. Fold the dough into thirds: Starting from one 12-inch side, fold one third of the dough over the middle third; fold the opposite third over both to make a 12 x 2 1/2-inch rectangular log. Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and refrigerate for 1 hour or until firm enough to slice (or place the dough in the freezer for 30 minutes).
5. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit. Cut the dough cross-wise into 1/4-inch-thick slices. Place the slices, cut-side down, on ungreased cookie sheets about 1 inch apart. Bake for 10 minutes or until very lightly browned around the edges. Remove from the oven and place on a wire rack to cool. Store in a tightly covered container and use within 1 week.
To keep waxed paper from slipping on the counter, dampen the counter slightly with a sponge before laying down the waxed paper.
